slices.title: Slice 55: Repository Context And Registry Foundation
slices.description:
## Goal

Introduce the reusable repository context and known-project registry needed for the workspace to operate on an explicitly selected Git repository rather than only the process launch directory.

## Requirements

- Add a reusable repository descriptor for local Git roots, including display name, resolved root path, last opened timestamp, and compact Pathfinder state status.
- Add a user-level recent/known repository registry stored outside target repositories.
- The registry must not require writing `.pathfinder/` into target repos.
- Repository records should be keyed by resolved Git root to avoid duplicate entries for subdirectories.
- Add validation helpers for local paths:
  - exists
  - directory
  - Git working tree or Git root
  - resolved Git root
  - readable Pathfinder state status where available
- Return clear statuses for non-directory, non-Git, uninitialized, repo-local initialized, personal/external initialized, and invalid state.
- Keep behavior local and deterministic; do not scan the filesystem for repositories.

## Technical Notes

- Prefer `packages/state` for registry persistence and state resolution.
- Put pure status/type helpers in `packages/core` only if shared across packages.
- Avoid local-server-specific types in reusable state code.
- Preserve existing state discovery for current working directory callers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A path inside a Git repository resolves to one canonical Git root record.
- Opening a repository adds or updates a recent-project record in user-level state.
- The registry survives process restarts.
- Repos without Pathfinder state can be represented without initializing them.
- Existing CLI/state tests for repo-local and personal state continue to pass.

## Checks

```bash
npm run typecheck
npm test
npm run lint --if-present
```

slices.title: Slice 56: Repository-Aware Workspace Server APIs
slices.description:
## Goal

Make the local workspace server route workspace APIs through an explicit selected repository context instead of assuming the launch directory forever.

## Requirements

- Add local-server selected repository context initialized from the launch Git root when available.
- Add repository-management API endpoints such as:

```http
GET /api/repositories/current
GET /api/repositories/recent
POST /api/repositories/open
```

- `POST /api/repositories/open` should accept a local path, validate it, resolve the Git root, update selected context, update recent projects, and return current workspace context.
- Existing workspace APIs should read from the selected repository context.
- Switching repositories should not require restarting `pathfinder workspace serve`.
- Error responses should remain JSON and consistent with existing local-server style.
- Existing current-repository startup behavior must remain compatible.

## Technical Notes

- Keep the selected repo context server-side; the browser should not construct filesystem behavior itself.
- The server may create per-request store/git adapters for the selected Git root.
- Avoid global mutable state that would make tests flaky; isolate context in the server instance.
- Do not add init behavior in this slice beyond reporting uninitialized status.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Starting the workspace from an initialized repo still loads that repo by default.
- Calling the open-repository API switches the workspace context to another initialized repo.
- Opening an uninitialized Git repo returns an uninitialized status instead of a generic server failure.
- Opening a non-Git folder returns a clear JSON error/status.
- Workstream, review, branch-review, feedback, and PR read APIs continue to work for the selected initialized repo.

## Checks

```bash
npm run typecheck
npm test
npm run lint --if-present
```

Smoke test:

```bash
pathfinder workspace serve --port 4783
```

Open another initialized repository through the API and verify `/api/workspace` reflects the new repository.

slices.title: Slice 57: Open Existing Project UI
slices.description:
## Goal

Add the browser UI for opening existing local projects and switching between recent repositories.

## Requirements

- Add an "Open existing project" action in the workspace shell.
- Show the current repository identity prominently enough to distinguish similarly named folders.
- Provide a reliable path-entry flow for opening a local repository.
- Show recent repositories from the server registry with status, display name, and last-opened context.
- Switch the workspace UI to the selected repository after a successful open action.
- Show clear UI states for invalid path, non-Git folder, uninitialized Git repo, initialized repo, and invalid Pathfinder state.
- Preserve existing workstream, slice, review, branch-review, artifact, and PR views once a repo is selected.
- Do not depend on a browser-only folder picker that cannot provide the absolute path required by the local server.
- A folder-picker affordance may be included only if backed by a local-server-compatible mechanism; otherwise keep the control labeled around path/recent-project opening.

## Technical Notes

- Keep UI calls through the local HTTP API.
- Avoid making the UI import state or git packages.
- Keep visual treatment quiet and work-focused: this is a project switcher, not a marketing landing page.
- Empty and error states should guide the user without exposing unnecessary internal state-root paths.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Users can open the project switcher from the workspace UI.
- Users can enter a path to a different initialized Git repo and load its Pathfinder workspace without restarting the server.
- Recent repositories appear after opening projects and can be reopened from the UI.
- Uninitialized repos route to the planned init state instead of pretending there is no workspace at all.
- Current review and workspace UI flows remain accessible after switching repositories.

## Checks

```bash
npm run typecheck
npm test
npm run lint --if-present
npm run build
```

Smoke test:

```bash
pathfinder workspace serve --port 4783
```

Open initialized, uninitialized, and invalid paths from the UI and verify state-specific messaging.

slices.title: Slice 58: UI Init Flow For Selected Repositories
slices.description:
## Goal

Let users initialize Pathfinder for a selected uninitialized Git repository from the workspace UI, using choices equivalent to the CLI init flow.

## Requirements

- Add a local-server init endpoint for the selected repository, such as:

```http
POST /api/repositories/init
```

- Reuse existing store initialization and agent bootstrap/install behavior rather than duplicating CLI-only rules.
- Support personal/external state initialization.
- Support repo-local state initialization.
- Surface supported user/agent integration choices where practical, matching CLI options such as Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, or all.
- Before writing anything, show what the selected mode may write:
  - external/user state
  - repo-local `.pathfinder/`
  - repo-local `AGENTS.md` block
  - user-level agent instructions
  - command wrapper files, if selected
- Require explicit confirmation before initialization.
- After successful init, reload the selected repository workspace context and update recent-project metadata.
- Preserve CLI init behavior and tests.

## Technical Notes

- UI init should be an orchestration layer over reusable state APIs.
- Do not make the browser run shell commands.
- Ensure JSON responses distinguish validation failures from write failures.
- Do not silently install every integration by default; make integration choices explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Selecting an uninitialized Git repo shows an init flow instead of a dead-end error.
- Initializing in personal mode creates external state and loads an empty Pathfinder workspace for that repo.
- Initializing in repo-local mode creates repo-local Pathfinder state only after confirmation.
- Optional agent integration writes happen only when selected.
- Failed initialization reports a clear JSON/UI error and does not leave the UI in a misleading loaded state.

## Checks

```bash
npm run typecheck
npm test
npm run lint --if-present
npm run build
```

Smoke test:

```bash
pathfinder workspace serve --port 4783
```

Open a temporary uninitialized Git repo from the UI, initialize it in personal mode, then verify the workspace loads.

slices.title: Slice 59: Repository Management Polish And Guardrails
slices.description:
## Goal

Polish repository-management guardrails so the workspace feels dependable when users switch among multiple local projects.

## Requirements

- Add repository status summaries to the project switcher and workspace rail.
- Add refresh behavior for the current repository status and recent-project entries.
- Handle moved or deleted recent repositories gracefully.
- Add a way to remove a repository from the recent list without deleting repo files or Pathfinder state.
- Ensure selected repo identity, state mode, and initialization status remain clear after server restart.
- Add defensive tests around path normalization, duplicate recent entries, invalid paths, and uninitialized repos.
- Update README/help documentation for multi-repo workspace behavior.
- Keep launch-directory compatibility documented.

## Technical Notes

- This is a polish and hardening slice after the core open/init flows exist.
- Do not add filesystem-wide discovery in this slice.
- Do not add cloud-backed project lists or remote provider integration.
- Keep recent-project deletion limited to the external registry entry.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Recent projects can be refreshed and removed from the UI.
- Moved/deleted repos show a clear unavailable state.
- Duplicate paths and subdirectory selections collapse to the resolved Git root entry.
- Documentation explains opening existing projects, recent projects, and init behavior from the UI.
- Existing single-repo workspace usage remains unchanged for users who never switch projects.

## Checks

```bash
npm run typecheck
npm test
npm run lint --if-present
npm run build
```

Smoke test:

```bash
pathfinder workspace serve --port 4783
```

Verify recent-project removal, refresh, invalid path handling, and restart persistence.
